definition: to develop gradually, especially from a simple to a more complicated form; to develop sth in this way | [biology] to develop over time, often many generations, into forms that are better adapted to survive changes in their environment
example: The idea evolved (derivative) from a drawing I discovered in the attic. | The three species evolved (derivative) from a single ancestor.

definition: to concentrate on sth very intensely, to the exclusion of other things.
example: instead of finally writing the paper that's due tomorrow you hyperfocus on grinding your game and then the day is over

definition: the fact of seeming to see or hear somebody/something that is not really there, especially because of illness or drugs ; something that is seen or heard when it is not really there
example: to have hallucinations

definition: in a way that shows no human influence, sensitivity, or character.
example: The majority of locals have left the area, leaving the new stadiums soullessly empty.

definition: [formal] to estimate sth or form an opinion about sth, using the facts that you have now and that are relevant to one situation and supposing that they will be relevant to the new one
example: The figures were obtained by extrapolating from past trends. (generalize)

definition: to contain sth, especially sth hidden or dangerous (in this context, to hold or contain life).
example: He harbored (fond) the suspicion that someone in the agency was spying for the enemy.

definition: not having had anything removed or changed
example: unfiltered apple juice

definition: The maximum number of people, animals, or crops that a particular area can support.
example: In some areas, deer populations have exceeded their carrying capacity on available habitat.

definition: extremely quickly.
example: In the blink of an eye, he was gone.

definition: To contain or keep sth/sb within.
example: They were accused of harboring (give refuge to) a fugitive.

definition: an assistant to a vicar (= a priest, who is in charge of the church or churches in a particular area) | sth that has some good parts and some bad ones
example: a curated library of short movies available online | Their investment plan shows the familiar curate's egg pattern of some bits doing well and others doing badly.

definition: a device or station that receives a signal and transmits it to another. to pass sth along.
example: I relayed (transmit) the news to the others.

definition: Relating to the careful organization of a complicated activity.
example: Assigning an armed federal officer to every commercial flight would be a logistical (logistic) nightmare.

definition: [formal, informal] a strong band of tissue in the body that connects bones and supports organs and keeps them in position
example: I've torn a ligament. (ligament)

definition: The small bones that form the spine (backbone).
example: "...all 33 vertebrae (backbone) should appear stacked in a straight line..."""

definition: food for horses and cows
example: forage crops (forage)

definition: [formal] chosen to do a job but not yet having officially started it
example: an interview with the director designate (rightful)

definition: twisted together or connected closely.
example: Once you get through the tutorial part, the habit journaling starts regularly interwoven (mixed) with examples, science breaks, and reflections.

definition: a sharp tool for making hollow areas in wood | a deep, narrow hole or cut in a surface
example: He has received complaints about price gouging, with some vets charging £50 per dose. | The massive wheels were digging great gouges in the road’s surface.

definition: persistently avoided, ignored, or rejected
example: "duke Ellington shunned (friendless) music lessons as a kid to focus on baseball and painting and drawing"""

definition: a person who follows a pursuit without serious commitment or knowledge
example: They are dabblers (nonprofessional) in African music rather than experts.

definition: moving by going from side to side, or changing direction frequently
example: "what is that can be difficult to talk about because sometimes it looks like meandering or zigzagging (zigzag) or keeping a broader view it can look like getting behind"""

definition: lacking imagination or foresight. failing to consider future consequences
example: It was a very shortsighted (lacking foresight) decision.

definition: not tied or limited to a particular thing.
example: The goats wandered untethered (unchained) down the rough tracks.

definition: to make a system, country, government, etc. become less well established or successful
example: Terrorist attacks were threatening to destabilize the government. (destabilize)

definition: [specialist] to use a method in an experiment, a piece of research, etc. that gives every item an equal chance of being considered; to put things in a random order
example: The randomized (randomize) study will compare students' achievement in different types of school.

definition: To have the right to speak at a public meeting or in a debate.
example: Silence, please, the prime minister has the floor.

definition: To be sth that is made or formed of various specific things.
example: The beauty of air travel consists (is composed) in its speed and ease.

definition: in order to help sb|as the representative of sb or instead of them|in order to help sb
example: He began a petition in behalf (side) of the farmers.|On behalf (side) of the department I would like to thank you all.|They campaigned on behalf (side) of asylum seekers.

definition: standards by which sth is judged, decided, or graded.
example: The main criteria (criterion) for selection are experience and academic achievement.

definition: [formal] according to a rule or the way that sb says that sth should be done
example: We acted in accordance (consonance) with my parents’ wishes.

definition: to say that sb has done sth wrong or is guilty of sth
example: She accused him of lying.

definition: HIV disease
example: the AIDS epidemic

definition: service counter
example: I asked the woman behind the counter if they had any postcards.

definition: reply proving wrong; reduce bad effects
example: Such arguments are not easily countered.|Businesses would like to see new laws to counter late payments of debts.

definition: burial site
example: We visited Grandma's grave.

definition: identification
example: You must carry ID at all times.

definition: narrow piece
example: a strip of material

definition: remove
example: I stripped and washed myself all over.|Strip off all the existing paint.|We had to strip out all the old wiring and start again.

definition: general health and happiness
example: We try to ensure the well-being of our employees. (well-being)

definition: based on your own ideas or opinions rather than facts and therefore sometimes unfair
example: Everyone's opinion is bound to be subjective (personal).

definition: the fact of seeming to see or hear somebody/something that is not really there, especially because of illness or drugs ; something that is seen or heard when it is not really there
example: to have hallucinations

definition: to enter a place in large numbers, especially in a way that causes damage or problems | to affect sth in an unpleasant or annoying way
example: Demonstrators invaded the government buildings. | Do the press have the right to invade her privacy in this way?

definition: to stop considering that sb/sth might be responsible for sth or chosen for sth | to defeat a person or a team so that they no longer take part in a competition, etc.
example: The police have eliminated (obviate) two suspects from their investigation. | All the English teams were eliminated (obviate) in the early stages of the competition.

definition: a thing that happens, often unexpectedly, as the result of sth else
example: One of the by-products of unemployment is an increase in crime.

definition: serious
example: The police have expressed grave concern about the missing child's safety.
